<div class="page">

# IPSEC en routers Mikrotik

\

Node 1

The configuration is made in GNS3 and is like the one in the figure:

Two Mikrotik routers with a trunk link on the 128.10.10.0/24 network. Lso
PCs located on another interface of each router (Site A 10.10.20.0/24 and
Site B 10.10.10.0/24)

Two Windox dockers, each connected to another interface of each router,
to access their management application, although you can
configure everything from terminal.

We begin to configure the router that gives access to Site_A.

Using Mikrotik's WinBox, once connected,

we can define the IPs of the interfaces, to do this, Menu
IP-\>Addresses:

eth1 128.10.10.1/24

eth2 10.10.20.1/24

eth3 192.168.0.1/24 (would not be necessary)

We open the menu, IP-\>IPSEC, and in the Profiles folder we prepare a
PROFILE that we will call IPSEC-to-site-b, with the image data:

 [![](../images/135-1.png)](data:image/png;base64,)

We select the PEERS folder and generate a peer with the name
IPSEC-site-b, with the data from the other end of the tunnel, as shown
indicates in the image:

[![](images/135-2.png)](data:image/png;base64,)

Next we select the Identities folder and create one,
selecting the peer created previously, specifying a password
which must be the same on both sides:

[![](images/135-3.png)](data:image/png;base64,)

It's time to create a Proposal, which we will call IKEv2-proposal, just like
picture shows:

[![](images/135-4.png)](data:image/png;base64,)

 

And all that remains is to create a Policy, indicating the networks that will have access to the
tunnel:

[![](images/135-5.png)](data:image/png;base64,)

We do not leave the menu, because we must activate the proposal created in the
previous step:

[![](images/135-6.png)](data:image/png;base64,)

All that remains is to introduce a rule in the Firewall with NAT. In
IP-\>firewall-\>NAT

 

We move on to configure the other side of the tunnel. In this case we will do it
from a Mikrotik console:

<div class="codebox">

    #Creamos el Profile
    /ip/ipsec/profile/add name=IPSEC-site-a enc-algorithm=aes-256 hash-algorithm=sha512 dh-group=modp2048
    #Creamos el Peer
    /ip/ipsec/peer/add name=IPSEC-site-a profile=IPSEC-site-a exchange-mode=ike2 address=128.10.10.1
     #Creamos la identidad 
    /ip ipsec identity add peer=IPSEC-site-a secret=tnb123
     #Creamos la Proposal
    /ip/ipsec/proposal/add name=IKEv2-Proposal auth-algorithms=sha512 enc-algorithms=aes-256-cbc pfs-grobc pfs-group=modp2048
    #Creamos la policy
    /ip/ipsec/policy/add peer=IPSEC-site-a tunnel=yes src-address=10.10.20.0/24 dst-address=10.10.10.0/24 proposal=IKEv2-Proposal action=encrypt

</div>

We can check what was entered:

 

<div class="codebox">

/ip/ipsec/export
    # 2025-10-03 19:32:14 by RouterOS 7.19.4
    # system id = CivrS0+NSyG
    #
    /ip ipsec profile
    add dh-group=modp2048 enc-algorithm=aes-256 hash-algorithm=sha512 name=IPSEC-site-a
    /ip ipsec peer
    add address=192.168.1.1/32 exchange-mode=ike2 name=IPSEC-site-a profile=IPSEC-site-a
    /ip ipsec proposal
    add auth-algorithms=sha512 enc-algorithms=aes-256-cbc name=IKEv2-Proposal pfs-group=modp2048
    /ip ipsec identity
    add peer=IPSEC-site-a
    /ip ipsec policy
    add dst-address=10.10.20.0/24 peer=IPSEC-site-a proposal=IKEv2-Proposal src-address=10.10.10.0/24 tunnel=yes

</div>

Finally, we have to add the NAT of the tunnel:

<div class="codebox">

/ip/firewall/nat/add chain=srcnat src-address=10.10.10.0/24 dst-address=10.10.20.0/24 action=accept
    To view the NAT rules:
    /ip/firewall/nat/print                                                                              
    Flags: X - disabled, I - invalid; D - dynamic
     0    chain=srcnat action=accept src-address=10.10.10.0/24 dst-address=10.10.20.0/24

</div>

To save settings:

<div class="codebox">

/system backup save name=config_backup

</div>

## Activar y Desactivar IPSEC

To disable the use of IPSEC, simply execute on both routers:

<div class="codebox">

/ip ipsec peer disable [find]
    /ip ipsec policy disable [find]

</div>

To activate we follow the reverse process:

<div class="codebox">

/ip ipsec peer enable [find]
    /ip ipsec policy enable [find]

</div>

Through Winbox you can do the same process, going to the
Policy and Peer tabs, select the rule and click the
Disable/Enable

</div>
